transformation_type: enantioselective dioxirane epoxidation of unfunctionalized alkenes
mechanism_type: ketone-catalyzed Oxone oxidation via chiral dioxirane oxygen transfer
common_conditions: pH-controlled buffered oxidation, often 0–25 °C with fructose-derived ketone catalyst
description_short: Chiral ketone-catalyzed epoxidation that uses Oxone-generated dioxiranes to furnish enantioenriched epoxides from alkenes.

mechanism_type: concerted zinc carbenoid transfer of CH2 to alkenes
common_conditions: zinc carbenoid generation from CH2I2 with Zn-Cu or Et2Zn, often low temperature to room temperature
description_short: Cyclopropanation of alkenes by zinc carbenoid methylene transfer with strong stereospecificity and useful allylic-alcohol direction.

reactant_pattern_text: aniline + glycerol or α,β-unsaturated carbonyl compound
common_conditions: strongly acidic oxidative conditions with heating
description_short: Classical quinoline-forming annulations of anilines with glycerol or enals/enones under acidic oxidative conditions.

transformation_type: intramolecular aryl migration by nucleophilic aromatic substitution
mechanism_type: base-promoted intramolecular SNAr through a five-membered transition state
reactant_pattern_text: activated diaryl ether/sulfide/sulfone or related biaryl substrate
common_conditions: strong base or thermal conditions depending on substrate activation
description_short: Intramolecular rearrangement in which an anionic nucleophile attacks an activated aryl ring ipso-position to transfer the aryl group.

mechanism_type: epoxide opening followed by solvent-controlled [1,4]-Brook rearrangement
product_pattern_text: 1,5-polyol fragment or advanced polyoxygenated coupling product
common_conditions: low-temperature lithiation and epoxide opening followed by Brook rearrangement, sometimes HMPA or DMPU mediated
description_short: A multicomponent linchpin process using silylated dithianes and epoxides to build symmetrical or unsymmetrical 1,5-polyol fragments.
